title: The Titan Speaks (Sort Of)
tagline: Oceanus communicated through the dolphins. The dolphins tried their best. The translation was imperfect.
hook_line: Poseidon arrived at breakfast holding a wet scroll. 'The Titan said something,' he said. 'The dolphins delivered the message.' He unrolled it. It said: WATER. HELLO. SKY VERY BIG. ARE YOU WELL. FISH. 'That's the full message,' Poseidon said.

scenes.title: The First Message
scenes.narration: Poseidon arrived at breakfast. He was wetter than usual, which was saying something. He was carrying a small scroll with bite marks on it. 'The Titan communicated,' he said. Everyone stopped eating. He unrolled the scroll. It said: WATER. HELLO. SKY VERY BIG. ARE YOU WELL. FISH. There was a pause. 'The dolphins translated,' Poseidon said. 'They tried their best.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Breakfast table at Little Olympus, the full ensemble gathered, morning light — Poseidon arriving, significantly wetter than usual, holding a small scroll with visible bite marks on the edges — unrolling it to reveal the message in large clear letters: WATER. HELLO. SKY VERY BIG. ARE YOU WELL. FISH. — the ensemble's reactions: Zeus staring at the scroll, Athena's pen hovering over her own scroll mid-write, Hercules reading it twice, Perseus tilting his head, Achilles frowning at FISH specifically, Artemis with an expression of acute observation — Archie looking at the scroll with an expression that suggests he may understand something — #FFD700 and morning light, the dolphin bite marks on the scroll visible in detail

scenes.title: Translating the Message
scenes.narration: Athena took the scroll. She looked at it for a long time. 'WATER is a greeting,' she said. 'It's what Oceanus is. It's saying: I am here.' She pointed to SKY VERY BIG. 'It's been underground for a long time. The sky is significant.' Then ARE YOU WELL. 'It's asking after us.' Then FISH. 'I don't know about the fish.' Poseidon raised his hand. 'I may have given it some fish while we were figuring out the message system,' he said. 'Possibly several fish.' 'How many fish,' Athena said. 'Some fish,' Poseidon said.
scenes.visual_prompt: Athena at the breakfast table with the scroll, working through it systematically — visual annotations appearing above each word as she interprets: WATER = greeting/self-identification, HELLO = greeting (two greetings, emphasis?), SKY VERY BIG = experiencing scale after underground confinement, ARE YOU WELL = inquiry about the group, FISH = ??? — then Poseidon's admission about the fish, his expression caught between dignity and sheepishness — Athena's specific look at him: 'how many fish' — everyone else watching Poseidon not answer the question directly — Archie with a knowing expression — #FFD700 and breakfast warmth, the annotation text in gentle colors

scenes.title: Crafting a Reply
scenes.narration: They decided to send a reply. The question was what you could send through dolphins. Athena started a list: words that dolphins could carry reliably, words that were simple enough to survive translation, words that were worth saying to an ancient Titan who had been underground for several thousand years. After some discussion, they sent: HELLO. WE ARE WELL. THE SKY IS NICE. WE ARE GLAD YOU ARE RESTING. Hercules added: THANK YOU FOR NOT DESTROYING THINGS. Zeus removed that one. Then put it back. Then removed it again.
scenes.visual_prompt: The group around the table working on the reply — Athena's scroll showing drafts and crossings-out — the evolution of the message visible: multiple attempts, words being added and removed — the Hercules THANK YOU FOR NOT DESTROYING THINGS entry visible with a strikethrough, then it appears again, then has a question mark — Zeus's hand hovering over it — the final message visible: HELLO. WE ARE WELL. THE SKY IS NICE. WE ARE GLAD YOU ARE RESTING. — Poseidon at the end saying he'll take it to the dolphins — everyone's body language showing the particular absurdity of composing a diplomatic communication to a Titan through dolphins — Archie watching the process, #FFD700 and collaborative warmth

scenes.title: Poseidon's Dolphin Briefing
scenes.narration: Poseidon went to the sea cliff to brief the dolphins. This took longer than expected. The dolphins were enthusiastic and kept offering suggestions. 'They want to add their own message,' Poseidon reported when he came back. 'I told them no. They added it anyway. I don't know what they said.' He handed Zeus the scroll. The dolphins' addition said: SPLASHING IS NICE. WE ALSO LIKE FISH. 'I respect their initiative,' Hercules said.
scenes.visual_prompt: Poseidon at the sea cliff talking to a group of cartoon dolphins — the dolphins visibly enthusiastic and animated, jumping and chattering while Poseidon tries to convey the message — Poseidon's expression of someone managing a very willing but imprecise communication team — then back at the group: Poseidon presenting the final transmitted message including the dolphin addition: SPLASHING IS NICE. WE ALSO LIKE FISH. appended to the bottom — the ensemble's reactions: Hercules genuinely respectful, Athena sighing, Zeus reading it twice — Archie looking at the dolphin addition with the expression of someone who finds this very reasonable — #FFD700 and sea-bright #00BFFF, dolphin energy cartoon style

scenes.title: The Second Message Arrives
scenes.narration: The next day, a reply arrived. The dolphins brought it in the afternoon. The scroll said: WE RECEIVED YOUR MESSAGE. THE SKY IS ALSO NICE FROM BELOW. RESTING IS WHAT I AM DOING. I HAVE A QUESTION. WHAT HAPPENED WHILE I WAS UNDERGROUND. FISH ALSO NICE. SPLASHING AGREED. It was the longest message yet. Athena read it four times. 'It's been underground for thousands of years,' she said quietly. 'It wants to know what it missed.' No one said anything for a moment. Archie blinked very slowly.
scenes.visual_prompt: The second scroll arriving via dolphin, wetter than the first — Athena reading it, the full message visible: WE RECEIVED YOUR MESSAGE. THE SKY IS ALSO NICE FROM BELOW. RESTING IS WHAT I AM DOING. I HAVE A QUESTION. WHAT HAPPENED WHILE I WAS UNDERGROUND. FISH ALSO NICE. SPLASHING AGREED. — Athena's quiet reading of it the fourth time — the group's reaction to 'what happened while I was underground': the weight of the question landing differently than the previous messages — this is no longer just funny, it's also genuinely moving — Zeus looking at his crown — Hercules looking at his hands — Perseus looking toward the west garden — Archie doing his slow blink — #FFD700 and something softer in the palette for this moment

scenes.title: Athena's Answer
scenes.narration: Athena started writing. It took her all afternoon. When she was done, she had filled three scrolls with everything she thought the Titan might want to know: what the world looked like now, what had changed, what was the same, what had been built, what had been lost. She wasn't sure how much would survive dolphin translation. She sent all three scrolls anyway. The dolphins came back empty-scrolled in an hour. At least they delivered them.
scenes.visual_prompt: Athena writing intensely at her desk, afternoon light, three scrolls visible — each one filling with text: the world as it is, what changed, what was built, what was lost — a sense of care in the composition, this is someone writing to explain everything to someone who missed thousands of years — then: Poseidon at the cliff with the three scrolls and the dolphins — the dolphins taking the scrolls, enthusiastically — then: the dolphins returning an hour later, empty — they delivered them — Athena receiving this news with a look that suggests she's trusting the process — Archie on her desk when she finishes writing, looking at the three scrolls with calm approval — #FFD700 and afternoon warm gold

scenes.title: Three Days Later
scenes.narration: Three days later, a message arrived. It was longer than any previous message. The scroll, when unrolled, ran from Poseidon's hands to the ground. It said, in the dolphins' best translation: THANK YOU FOR THE INFORMATION. I HAVE MANY MORE QUESTIONS. I WILL SEND THEM SLOWLY BECAUSE THE DOLPHINS GET TIRED. I LIKED THE PART ABOUT THE BUILDING WITH THE PILLARS. DID THE RIVER STILL FLOW NORTH. FISH. The part about the river. They hadn't mentioned a river. 'It remembered a river from before,' Athena said softly. 'From a very long time before.' Zeus's crown was straight. For almost six seconds.
scenes.visual_prompt: The long scroll arriving — Poseidon holding it as it unfurls dramatically to the ground — the message visible: the full THANK YOU message running down the scroll — the ensemble gathered to read it, moving along the scroll as it unfurls — the specific line 'DID THE RIVER STILL FLOW NORTH' highlighted as the detail none of them put in the letter — Athena's 'it remembered a river from before' moment: her expression going very quiet — and then: Zeus, visible in the frame — his crown straight — visibly straight — for six seconds — the longest it's been straight all season — #FFD700 and warm light, the scroll dramatic and comic and genuinely moving at once

viral_hook: The ancient Titan is communicating through dolphins. The dolphins are doing their best. The latest message asked if a river still flows north. They didn't mention any river in their letter. The Titan is asking about something it remembered from thousands of years ago. Zeus's crown was straight for six seconds.
highlight_note: The second message — 'What happened while I was underground' — the moment the dolphin translation comedy becomes genuinely moving. The Titan has been underground for thousands of years and is asking what it missed.
short_title: The Titan Asked What It Missed While It Was Underground
rewatch_bait: Scene 1 — the dolphin bite marks on the first scroll. Count them. There are more bite marks than words in the message. The dolphins debated which words to use.
youtube_title: The ancient Titan is texting through dolphins. It wants to know what it missed. 🐬 | Little Olympus S2 EP6
lesson: Asking 'what did I miss?' takes courage — it admits you were gone and things happened without you. The Titan has been underground for thousands of years and still asked.
arc_notes: Titan-Dolphin communication system established. Running joke launched: the dolphins are trying their best, translation is imperfect, they add their own content. The river question in scene 7 is the emotional depth under the comedy: the Titan remembers something from before it went underground. Athena's three scrolls will be referenced in EP011 (What the Dolphins Know). Zeus crown straight for 6 seconds: longest of the series so far, specifically at the Titan's question about the river.
